title: Bakers
description: Mix and bake ingredients to produce breads, rolls, cookies, cakes, pies, pastries, or other baked goods.

tasks.text: Check products for quality, and identify damaged or expired goods.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set oven temperatures, and place items into hot ovens for baking.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Combine measured ingredients in bowls of mixing, blending, or cooking machinery.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place dough in pans, molds, or on sheets, and bake in production ovens or on grills.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set time and speed controls for mixing machines, blending machines, or steam kettles so that ingredients will be mixed or cooked according to instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Measure or weigh flour or other ingredients to prepare batters, doughs, fillings, or icings, using scales or graduated containers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Observe color of products being baked, and adjust oven temperatures, humidity, or conveyor speeds accordingly.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check the quality of raw materials to ensure that standards and specifications are met.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check equipment to ensure that it meets health and safety regulations, and perform maintenance or cleaning, as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Adapt the quantity of ingredients to match the amount of items to be baked.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply glazes, icings, or other toppings to baked goods, using spatulas or brushes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Decorate baked goods, such as cakes or pastries.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Roll, knead, cut, or shape dough to form sweet rolls, pie crusts, tarts, cookies, or other products.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Order or receive supplies or equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare or maintain inventory or production records.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.
Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop new recipes for baked goods.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
